1	# doc_classifier_gpu — GPU Embedding Document Classifier
2	
3	![logo](logo.gif)
4	
5	[![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.10-blue?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/)
6	[![PyTorch](https://img.shields.io/badge/PyTorch-2.13.0.dev%2Bcu130-ee4c2c?logo=pytorch&logoColor=white)](https://pytorch.org/)
7	[![CUDA](https://img.shields.io/badge/CUDA-13.0-76b900?logo=nvidia&logoColor=white)](https://developer.nvidia.com/cuda-toolkit)
8	[![GPU](https://img.shields.io/badge/GPU-RTX%205080%20Blackwell%20sm__120-76b900?logo=nvidia&logoColor=white)](https://www.nvidia.com/)
9	[![sentence-transformers](https://img.shields.io/badge/embed-bge--m3-orange?logo=huggingface&logoColor=white)](https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-m3)
10	[![EasyOCR](https://img.shields.io/badge/OCR-EasyOCR-blue)](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR)
11	[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-Linux-lightgrey?logo=linux&logoColor=white)](https://ubuntu.com/)
12	
13	**Session:** 2026-05-30_092836 · **Script:** `doc_classifier_gpu.py`  
14	Predecessor (TF-IDF baseline): [`../2026-05-30_091736_doc-classifier/`](../2026-05-30_091736_doc-classifier/)
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Quick Start
19	
20	```bash
21	# 1. Create venv and install Blackwell-compatible PyTorch (cu130 nightly required for sm_120)
22	python3 -m venv ~/doc-clf-gpu-env
23	source ~/doc-clf-gpu-env/bin/activate
24	pip install --pre torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cu130
25	
26	# 2. Verify RTX 5080 is visible (must print True (12, 0))
27	python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_capability(0))"
28	
29	# 3. Install remaining deps
30	pip install sentence-transformers easyocr pymupdf scikit-learn joblib pdfplumber python-docx striprtf
31	
32	# 4. Train (sub-folders of training_root become class labels)
33	CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python doc_classifier_gpu.py train /path/to/training_root -m model.joblib
34	
35	# 5. Classify
36	CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict /path/to/new_doc.pdf -m model.joblib
37	```
38	
39	---
40	
41	## Overview
42	
43	This is the semantic upgrade to the TF-IDF baseline. Instead of word-frequency vectors,
44	each document is embedded by a transformer encoder that understands meaning — so two
45	documents using completely different vocabulary but describing the same concept land
46	close together in vector space.
47	
48	**Use this when:**
49	- Categories share overlapping vocabulary (e.g. all folders have military jargon but differ by topic/role)
50	- The TF-IDF baseline plateaus at accuracy you don't trust
51	- You have an idle RTX 5080 with ~16 GB of free VRAM
52	
53	**Stick with the TF-IDF baseline when:**
54	- Categories differ purely by vocabulary (legal vs. financial vs. personal) — TF-IDF will
55	  match or beat this script and runs in seconds with no model download
56	- You need a quick sanity-check run with no GPU dependencies
57	
58	### Pipeline architecture
59	
60	![Training Pipeline](diagrams/arch1_training_pipeline.png)
61	
62	*Full data flow: militia-copy corpus → parallel CPU extraction → GPU OCR → chunk → bge-m3 embed → mean-pool → LogisticRegression → model bundle*
63	
64	---
65	
66	## Blackwell PyTorch install
67	
68	The RTX 5080 is Blackwell (compute capability **sm_120**). Stock PyTorch wheels top out at
69	sm_90 — running them will either crash with `no kernel image available` or silently fall
70	back to CPU. The cu130 nightly wheel ships the required sm_120 kernels.
71	
72	```bash
73	pip install --pre torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cu130
74	
75	# Must print: True (12, 0)
76	python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_capability(0))"
77	```
78	
79	Verified working: `torch 2.13.0.dev20260521+cu130` on driver 580 / CUDA 13.0.
80	
81	---
82	
83	## GPU stack
84	
85	Both models are loaded onto GPU 0 at startup and remain resident for the duration of training.
86	
87	![GPU Model Stack](diagrams/arch3_gpu_model_stack.png)
88	
89	| Model | VRAM | Purpose |
90	|-------|------|---------|
91	| `BAAI/bge-m3` | ~2,200 MiB | Document embedding — 8192-token context, 1024-dim output |
92	| EasyOCR (en) | ~200 MiB | OCR for scanned PDFs and image files |
93	| **Total in use** | **~9,400 MiB** | of 16,303 MiB available on RTX 5080 |
94	
95	First run downloads bge-m3 (~2.2 GB) to `~/.cache/huggingface/` and EasyOCR weights
96	(~100 MB) to `~/.EasyOCR/`. Both are cached; subsequent runs load from disk.
97	
98	---
99	
100	## GPU pinning
101	
102	Worlock has two GPUs:
103	- **GPU 0 — RTX 5080 (16 GB, Blackwell)** — use this one
104	- **GPU 1 — RTX 3080 (10 GB)** — display attached, ~557 MiB in use by compositor
105	
106	Always set `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0` so training doesn't compete with the desktop for VRAM.
107	
108	---
109	
110	## Parallel CPU extraction
111	
112	Text extraction is parallelized across CPU cores using `ProcessPoolExecutor`. On worlock
113	(16 physical cores / 32 threads) the default is 14 workers, giving ~14× faster extraction
114	for CPU-readable files compared to sequential processing.
115	
116	![Extraction Worker Pool](diagrams/arch2_extraction_worker_pool.png)
117	
118	**Three-phase extraction per class:**
119	
120	1. **Phase A — Parallel CPU** (`ProcessPoolExecutor`, 14 workers): each worker calls
121	   `extract_text()` with no OCR. pdfminer C extensions (via Pillow, when PDFs contain
122	   embedded images) run in separate interpreter processes, bypassing the GIL.
123	
124	2. **Phase B — Sequential CPU fallback**: if a worker crashes (e.g. Pillow segfault on a
125	   corrupt image), a `BrokenProcessPool` guard catches it and retries those files
126	   sequentially. The training run continues rather than crashing.
127	
128	3. **Phase C — Sequential GPU OCR**: files that returned no text (scanned PDFs, `.jpg`,
129	   `.png`, etc.) are OCR'd via EasyOCR + PyMuPDF on GPU 0.
130	
131	> **Note on ThreadPoolExecutor:** an earlier version used `ThreadPoolExecutor` to avoid
132	> the `BrokenProcessPool` crash. This was incorrect — `pdfminer`, `pdfplumber`, and
133	> `pypdf` are pure Python with no `.so` files; the GIL serializes threads and eliminates
134	> any parallelism. `ProcessPoolExecutor` with exception resilience is the correct approach.
135	> See [incident notes](#incident-notes) and `diagrams/incident1_brokenpoolprocess_fault_tree`.
136	
137	---
138	
139	## Usage
140	
141	```bash
142	# Train — sub-folders of training_root become the class labels
143	CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python doc_classifier_gpu.py train \
144	  /path/to/training_root -m militia.joblib
145	
146	# Classify a single file
147	CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict \
148	  /path/to/new_doc.pdf -m militia.joblib
149	
150	# Classify every file in a folder
151	CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict \
152	  /path/to/unlabelled_folder/ -m militia.joblib
153	```
154	
155	Output: top three candidate classes with confidence percentages per file.
156	
157	```
158	fieldmanual_007.pdf                      -> strategy 88%, C2 7%, Spec_ops 5%
159	patrol_photo.jpg                         -> camps 61%, Forest_hiking 24%, geography 15%
160	unknown_scan.pdf                         -> <no extractable text>
161	```
162	
163	Wide margin → high confidence. Tight spread → document is ambiguous between categories,
164	or those categories need more training examples.
165	
166	---
167	
168	## Key flags
169	
170	| Flag | Default | Notes |
171	|------|---------|-------|
172	| `--embed-model` | `BAAI/bge-m3` | 8192-token context. Lighter: `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` (fast), `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (tiny) |
173	| `--chunk-chars` | `4000` | Characters per chunk before mean-pooling. Lower for short docs |
174	| `--workers` | `cpu_count-2` (max 16) | Parallel CPU extraction processes. Tune down if memory-constrained |
175	| `-m` / `--model` | `model.joblib` | Output path for trained model bundle |
176	
177	`--embed-model` and `--chunk-chars` are **train-time parameters** baked into the saved
178	bundle — you don't pass them at predict time, they're loaded automatically.
179	
180	---
181	
182	## How chunking works
183	
184	Long documents (PDFs especially) exceed any encoder's context window. The script splits
185	each document into overlapping 4000-character chunks with a 200-character overlap, embeds
186	each chunk independently via bge-m3, then **mean-pools** them into a single document vector.
187	
188	This matters for long documents: a 40-page field manual has important content on page 1
189	and page 38. A naive truncation approach would drop page 38 entirely. Mean-pooling preserves
190	signal from the full document at the cost of slightly blurring page-level detail. The 200-char
191	overlap prevents sentences split at a chunk boundary from losing context in both halves.
192	
193	---
194	
195	## Supported formats
196	
197	| Format | Extraction method |
198	|--------|-------------------|
199	| `.pdf` with text layer | PyMuPDF/fitz (CPU) — handles embedded images safely via MuPDF |
200	| `.pdf` scanned / image-only | PyMuPDF render → EasyOCR (GPU) |
201	| `.jpg` `.jpeg` `.png` `.webp` `.tiff` `.bmp` | EasyOCR direct (GPU) |
202	| `.txt` `.md` `.rst` `.csv` `.log` `.json` `.xml` `.html` `.eml` | `read_text()` (CPU) |
203	| `.docx` | python-docx (CPU) |
204	| `.rtf` | striprtf (CPU) |
205	| `.doc` (old binary format) | ❌ needs `antiword` or `textract` |
206	
207	Files that yield no text after all extraction attempts are printed as `(skipped)` and
208	excluded from training. Check stderr for worker-level error details.
209	
210	---
211	
212	## Model bundle format
213	
214	The saved `.joblib` file is a plain dict — not a sklearn Pipeline:
215	
216	```python
217	{
218	    "clf": LogisticRegression(...),   # trained classifier
219	    "embed_model": "BAAI/bge-m3",    # encoder name — reloaded from cache at predict time
220	    "chunk_chars": 4000,             # chunking param
221	}
222	```
223	
224	Portability: moving the bundle to another machine requires either internet access on first
225	`predict` (to download the embedding model), or copying `~/.cache/huggingface/` and
226	`~/.EasyOCR/` alongside it.
227	
228	---
229	
230	## Interpreting CV accuracy
231	
232	Cross-validated accuracy is computed on held-out folds and reflects real generalisation,
233	not memorisation. Roughly:
234	
235	- **< 15 docs/class** → CV score is noise; add more examples before trusting the number
236	- **15–50 docs/class** → meaningful signal; score guides whether to continue or escalate
237	- **50+ docs/class** → reliable; if accuracy is still mediocre, consider encoder fine-tuning
238	
239	The script adapts fold count to `min(5, smallest_class_size)` and skips CV entirely if
240	any class has fewer than 2 documents.
241	
242	---
243	
244	## When to escalate to fine-tuning
245	
246	Embedding + LR is the right tool up to roughly **50 documents per class**. Above that,
247	with still-mediocre accuracy, fine-tuning the encoder end-to-end pulls ahead — the RTX
248	5080's 16 GB handles a base-size encoder (ModernBERT-base, ~150M params) comfortably.
249	
250	Upgrade path:
251	1. Replace `get_encoder()` with a `transformers` AutoModel + AutoTokenizer
252	2. Add a training loop (HuggingFace Trainer or plain PyTorch)
253	3. Keep the same `extract_text()`, `chunk_text()`, and `embed_documents()` logic
254	
255	---
256	
257	## Incident notes
258	
259	> **BrokenProcessPool crash — 2026-05-30**
260	>
261	> Training on the Liberated_manuals class (706 files) crashed with
262	> `concurrent.futures.process.BrokenProcessPool`. Root cause: Pillow's C extension
263	> (`_imaging.so`) segfaulted on a corrupt embedded image inside a PDF, killing the worker
264	> process and poisoning all pending futures. This was **not OOM** — 101 GB RAM was free.
265	>
266	> Fixed by adding a two-level exception guard: per-future `try/except` for individual
267	> worker failures, plus an outer `except BrokenProcessPool` that collects unfinished files
268	> and retries them sequentially. The training run now survives a worker segfault.
269	
270	![BrokenProcessPool Fault Tree](diagrams/incident1_brokenpoolprocess_fault_tree.png)
271	
272	---
273	
274	## Script evolution
275	
276	![Script Version Evolution](diagrams/evol1_script_versions.png)
277	
278	| Version | Commit | Key change |
279	|---------|--------|------------|
280	| v1 | `f82cd48` | Baseline: extract all → embed all. GPU idle during extraction. |
281	| v2 | `d138ab5` | Interleaved per-class extract→embed. Per-file verbose output. |
282	| v3 | `d138ab5` | EasyOCR + PyMuPDF GPU OCR. `--workers` flag. `_extract_class()`. |
283	| v4 | `d138ab5` | `ProcessPoolExecutor` — true CPU parallelism. **Crashed.** |
284	| v5 | `8c0c8a6` | `ThreadPoolExecutor` — wrong fix, GIL serializes pure-Python pdfminer. |
285	| v6 | `e2f2d30` | `ProcessPoolExecutor` restored + `BrokenProcessPool` resilience. Insufficient for Liberated_manuals (98% image PDFs). |
286	| v7 | `7c4380d` | `rich` TUI: Progress bars, GPU stat lines, Panel headers, summary Table. |
287	| v8 | `b0ea134` | **Current.** pdfplumber → PyMuPDF (fitz). `ProcessPoolExecutor` → `Pool(maxtasksperchild=1)`. Eliminates Pillow segfaults. |
288	
289	---
290	
291	## File tree
292	
293	```
294	2026-05-30_092836_doc-classifier-gpu/
295	├── doc_classifier_gpu.py       # main script (v6)
296	├── README.md                   # this file
297	├── SESSION.md                  # session log, incidents, resume command
298	└── diagrams/
299	    ├── arch1_training_pipeline.(dot|png|svg)
300	    ├── arch2_extraction_worker_pool.(dot|png|svg)
301	    ├── arch3_gpu_model_stack.(dot|png|svg)
302	    ├── evol1_script_versions.(dot|png|svg)
303	    └── incident1_brokenpoolprocess_fault_tree.(dot|png|svg)
304	```
305	
306	Related session: [`../2026-05-30_091736_doc-classifier/`](../2026-05-30_091736_doc-classifier/) — TF-IDF baseline
307

1	# How-To Guide — doc_classifier_gpu
2	
3	Operational reference for training, classifying, and sorting document collections
4	using the GPU embedding pipeline.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 0. Prerequisites
9	
10	```bash
11	# Activate the venv (required for every operation)
12	source ~/doc-clf-gpu-env/bin/activate
13	cd ~/Documents/claude_creations/2026-05-30_092836_doc-classifier-gpu/
14	```
15	
16	Verify GPU availability:
17	```bash
18	python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.device_count(), 'GPU(s)')"
19	nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,name,memory.free --format=csv,noheader
20	```
21	
22	---
23	
24	## 1. Train a new model
25	
26	### Prepare your corpus
27	
28	Each immediate sub-folder of the training root becomes a class label:
29	```
30	training_root/
31	├── strategy/       ← class "strategy"
32	├── medical/        ← class "medical"
33	└── weapons/        ← class "weapons"
34	```
35	
36	Minimum ~15 documents per class for reliable CV accuracy. Supported formats:
37	`.pdf`, `.jpg`, `.png`, `.webp`, `.txt`, `.md`, `.docx`, `.rtf`, `.eml`, `.xml`, `.html`
38	
39	### Launch training (in a visible xterm)
40	
41	```bash
42	DISPLAY=:0 xterm -title "training" -fa 'Monospace' -fs 11 -geometry 130x55 -e bash -c '
43	source ~/doc-clf-gpu-env/bin/activate
44	cd ~/Documents/claude_creations/2026-05-30_092836_doc-classifier-gpu
45	python doc_classifier_gpu.py train /path/to/corpus -m my_model.joblib
46	echo; echo "--- done --- press Enter ---"; read
47	' &
48	```
49	
50	The script automatically selects the GPU with the most free VRAM. No need to set
51	`CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` manually — `_pick_device()` handles it.
52	
53	### Key training flags
54	
55	| Flag | Default | Notes |
56	|------|---------|-------|
57	| `--embed-model` | `BAAI/bge-m3` | Lighter: `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` |
58	| `--chunk-chars` | `4000` | Lower for short docs |
59	| `--workers` | `cpu_count-2` | Parallel CPU extraction processes |
60	
61	### Read the output
62	
63	- **CV accuracy** at the end is your honest accuracy estimate (held-out folds)
64	- A wide confidence spread (e.g. 0.88 ± 0.04) means the model is reliable
65	- Classes with < 15 docs are flagged — add more documents before trusting them
66	
67	---
68	
69	## 2. Use the interactive wizard
70	
71	The wizard walks you through environment check → training → inference step by step:
72	
73	```bash
74	python wizard.py
75	```
76	
77	Modes: Train / Classify / Inspect / Quit. All steps explained inline.
78	
79	---
80	
81	## 3. Classify a single document
82	
83	```bash
84	python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict /path/to/document.pdf -m my_model.joblib
85	```
86	
87	Output: table showing top-3 predicted classes with confidence percentages.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 4. Classify a folder of documents
92	
93	```bash
94	python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict /path/to/folder/ -m my_model.joblib
95	```
96	
97	Processes all files recursively. Results are printed as a Table, one row per file.
98	Pipe to a file for large collections:
99	
100	```bash
101	python doc_classifier_gpu.py predict /path/to/folder/ -m my_model.joblib > results.txt
102	```
103	
104	---
105	
106	## 5. Bulk-sort a large collection (sort_docs.py)
107	
108	For large collections (thousands of files), use `sort_docs.py` which provides
109	progress bars, GPU stats, and a CSV report.
110	
111	### Step 1 — Report-only preview (no file operations)
112	
113	```bash
114	python sort_docs.py /path/to/source/ \
115	    --model militia.joblib \
116	    --output ./sorted \
117	    --mode report \
118	    --threshold 0.60
119	```
120	
121	Review `sorted/sort_report.csv` to see the classification distribution. Files below
122	60% confidence go to `_review/`.
123	
124	### Step 2 — Copy files into class folders
125	
126	```bash
127	python sort_docs.py /path/to/source/ \
128	    --model militia.joblib \
129	    --output ./sorted \
130	    --mode copy \
131	    --threshold 0.60
132	```
133	
134	Creates `sorted/<class>/filename.pdf` for each classified file.
135	
136	### Step 3 — Symlink instead of copy (saves disk space)
137	
138	```bash
139	python sort_docs.py /path/to/source/ \
140	    --model militia.joblib \
141	    --output ./sorted \
142	    --mode symlink \
143	    --threshold 0.60
144	```
145	
146	Source files stay in place; `sorted/` contains symlinks. Requires the source
147	volume to remain mounted.
148	
149	### sort_docs.py flags
150	
151	| Flag | Default | Notes |
152	|------|---------|-------|
153	| `--mode` | `copy` | `copy` \| `symlink` \| `report` |
154	| `--threshold` | `0.40` | Files below this confidence → `_review/` |
155	| `--workers` | `cpu_count-2` | Parallel CPU extraction workers |
156	| `--batch` | `256` | Documents per GPU embedding batch |
157	
158	---
159	
160	## 6. Inspect an existing model
161	
162	```bash
163	python -c "
164	import joblib, numpy as np
165	b = joblib.load('militia.joblib')
166	clf = b['clf']
167	classes = clf.classes_
168	norms = np.linalg.norm(clf.coef_, axis=1)
169	print(f'{len(classes)} classes  embed={b[\"embed_model\"]}  chunk={b[\"chunk_chars\"]}')
170	print()
171	for cls, n in sorted(zip(classes, norms), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:10]:
172	    print(f'  {cls:<35} norm={n:.3f}')
173	"
174	```
175	
176	Higher weight norm = more distinctive class in embedding space.
177	
178	---
179	
180	## 7. Two GPUs simultaneously
181	
182	Training on GPU 0 + classification on GPU 1 works automatically:
183	
184	```bash
185	# Terminal 1: training (auto-selects GPU 0 if free)
186	python doc_classifier_gpu.py train /corpus -m model.joblib
187	
188	# Terminal 2: sort run (auto-selects GPU 1 when GPU 0 is busy)
189	python sort_docs.py /source -m model.joblib --output ./sorted --mode report
190	```
191	
192	The `_pick_device()` function probes free VRAM on all GPUs and picks the best one.
193	No `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` needed.
194	
195	---
196	
197	## 8. Troubleshooting
198	
199	| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
200	|---------|-------------|-----|
201	| `torch.OutOfMemoryError` at model load | GPU busy (training running) | Script now auto-selects next GPU |
202	| `BrokenProcessPool` | C extension segfault in worker (old pdfplumber code) | Use v10+ code (PyMuPDF) |
203	| `(skipped)` for most files | Scanned PDFs, no text layer | EasyOCR handles these via GPU OCR |
204	| Many `worker error — retrying sequentially` | Old pdfplumber code on image-heavy PDFs | Upgrade to v10 (PyMuPDF) |
205	| `console.print` TypeError in except block | `stderr=True` is not a rich param | Already fixed in v9 |
206	| `._filename` files crashing EasyOCR | macOS resource forks in file list | Already filtered in v9 |
207	| CV accuracy not printed | All classes have < 2 docs (can't split) | Add more training examples |
208	| Low accuracy on a class | < 15 docs in that class | Add documents or merge with similar class |
209

```python
def sort_wizard():
    section("Bulk Sort — sort_docs.py")

    if not SORTER.exists():
        err(f"sort_docs.py not found at [cyan]{SORTER}[/]")
        return

    # source
    console.print()
    source_str = Prompt.ask("  [bold]Source directory[/] (folder of files to classify)")
    source = Path(source_str.strip())
    if not source.is_dir():
        err(f"Not a directory: {source}")
        return

    file_count = sum(1 for f in source.rglob("*")
                     if f.is_file() and not f.name.startswith("._"))
    ok(f"[cyan]{source.name}[/]: [green]{file_count:,}[/] files")

    # model
    console.print()
    model_str = Prompt.ask("  [bold]Model file[/]", default=str(DEFAULT_MODEL))
    model_path = Path(model_str)
    if not model_path.exists():
        err(f"Model not found: {model_path}")
        return

    # output
    default_out = SCRIPT_DIR / (source.name + "_sorted")
    output_str  = Prompt.ask("  [bold]Output directory[/]", default=str(default_out))
    output = Path(output_str)

    # mode
    console.print()
    console.print("  [bold]Mode[/]")
    console.print("    [1] [cyan]report[/]   — classify only, write CSV, no file operations (preview)")
    console.print("    [2] [cyan]copy[/]     — copy files into class sub-folders (safe, uses disk space)")
    console.print("    [3] [cyan]symlink[/]  — create symlinks (space-efficient; source must stay mounted)")
    mode_choice = Prompt.ask("    Select", choices=["1","2","3"], default="1")
    mode = {"1": "report", "2": "copy", "3": "symlink"}[mode_choice]

    # threshold
    console.print()
    console.print("  [bold]Confidence threshold[/]")
    info("Files below threshold → _review/ folder. Higher = more files in _review/.")
    info("0.40 = sort most files   0.60 = sort high-confidence only   0.80 = very strict")
    threshold = Prompt.ask("    --threshold", default="0.60")

    # workers / batch
    default_workers = min(16, max(1, (os.cpu_count() or 4) - 2))
    workers = IntPrompt.ask("  [bold]--workers[/] (CPU extraction)", default=default_workers)
    batch   = IntPrompt.ask("  [bold]--batch[/] (docs per GPU embedding batch)", default=256)

    cmd = (
        f"{PYTHON} {SORTER} \"{source}\" "
        f"-m \"{model_path}\" "
        f"-o \"{output}\" "
        f"--mode {mode} "
        f"--threshold {threshold} "
        f"--workers {workers} "
        f"--batch {batch}"
    )

    console.print()
    console.print("  Command:\n")
    console.print(Panel(f"[green]{cmd}[/]", border_style="green", padding=(0, 2)))
    console.print()

    info(f"Estimated time for {file_count:,} files:")
    info(f"  Extraction: ~{file_count // (workers * 60) + 1} min ({workers} workers)")
    info(f"  Embedding:  ~{file_count // 1500 + 1} min (bge-m3 on GPU)")
    info(f"  Output:     {output}")
    console.print()

    if not Confirm.ask("  Launch sort run now?", default=True):
        console.print("  [dim]Cancelled.[/]")
        return

    xterm_cmd = (
        f'DISPLAY=:0 xterm -title "sort_docs — {source.name}" '
        f'-fa "Monospace" -fs 11 -geometry 130x55 '
        f'-e bash -c \'source {VENV_DIR}/bin/activate; {cmd}; '
        f'echo; echo "--- done (exit $?) --- press Enter to close ---"; read\' &'
    )
    os.system(xterm_cmd)
    ok("Sort run launched in xterm.")

    if mode == "report":
        info(f"Results will be in [cyan]{output}/sort_report.csv[/]")
        info("Review the CSV, then rerun with --mode copy or symlink to actually sort files.")
```
